title: UP Deputy CM Claims 25-26 Samajwadi Party MPs Ready to Defect
summary: UP Deputy CM Keshav Prasad Maurya claimed a mass defection of Samajwadi Party Lok Sabha MPs is imminent ahead of the 2027 state polls. Akhilesh Yadav dismissed the claim as a BJP pressure tactic. No MP has been named, and no formal communication of any defection has surfaced.

coverages.headline: 25-26 Samajwadi Party MPs ready to defect, claims Uttar Pradesh deputy chief minister
coverages.excerpt: UP Deputy CM Keshav Prasad Maurya claimed a mass SP Lok Sabha defection is imminent ahead of 2027 state polls; Akhilesh Yadav dismissed it as BJP pressure tactics.
coverages.bias_notes: This story follows almost the identical structural pattern as the Shiv Sena (UBT) 'Operation Tiger' defection claims from earlier in June — a ruling-alliance leader claims a large, specific number of opposition MPs are ready to defect, naming no individuals, followed by a flat denial from the opposition leader. Scroll.in reports the claim and the denial in the same breath, which is appropriately balanced, but doesn't flag the pattern-matching with the Maharashtra story or ask why specific MP names are never attached to these claims when they're made. A claim with this structure — vague on specifics, repeated across states, timed ahead of elections — deserves to be treated as a political tactic worth naming as such, not merely reported as a factual dispute between two leaders.
